title: Minecraft, But It Gets Faster
description: Increases the tick speed exponentially, starting at 1 per minute.
body:
Increases the tick rate of Minecraft by 1 every 4,000 ticks (200 seconds), including the rate of change itself. So at 40 TPS, it's every 100 seconds, and so on. Can you beat Minecraft before it's too fast?

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/5S1euVWFo7k" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

This data pack **MUST BE INSTALLED ON A SERVER** (in the `world/datapacks` folder), and you have to change `function-permission-level` in `server.properties` to 3. This is because the `/tick` command requires permission level 3, which data pack functions don't normally have.

To start, run `/function tickspeed:start`.

Tick speed resets whenever the world is reloaded.

If you make a video with this data pack, please link back here - thank you!
